releases.headline: Accessibility, cookie policy, and landing-page updates
releases.summary: Improves keyboard and screen-reader accessibility across interactive elements, updates the cookie policy to cover optional analytics scripts admins may enable, and refreshes the landing page with current deployment platforms and feature surfaces.

items.title: Keyboard focus rings on interactive elements
items.body: `:focus-visible` styles are now applied across buttons, links, and form controls so keyboard users have a consistent focus indicator on every interactive surface.

items.title: `aria-label` on the logged-out theme toggle
items.body: The theme toggle in the logged-out header was previously unlabeled for assistive tech; it now exposes a descriptive accessible name.

items.title: `aria-hidden` on the decorative scroll progress indicator
items.body: Marks the scroll progress bar as decorative so screen readers do not announce its constantly-changing percentage value.

items.title: Deployment platform grid on the landing page
items.body: Surfaces Kubernetes, Podman, Proxmox/LXD, Terraform, Ansible, and Ubuntu/Debian as first-class install paths alongside the existing Docker option.

items.title: Upload Limits and Admin Overview cards in the Customer Stash section
items.body: Two new feature cards explain configurable upload caps and the admin overview dashboard so prospective operators see those capabilities before installing.

items.title: Cookie policy covers optional analytics scripts
items.body: Updated to account for the optional Plausible, Umami, and Matomo integrations admins can enable on public pages, including the cookies (or lack thereof) each one sets.

items.title: Application-layer crypto claim clarified
items.body: The landing-page crypto claim now specifies application-layer encryption explicitly so it is not read as a claim about the TLS cipher suites, which use standard transport algorithms.

items.title: Settings count updated from 40+ to 70+
items.body: Reflects the actual size of the admin settings surface after the v1.6.0 schema work.

items.title: Landing-page button styles aligned
items.body: The bottom GitHub call-to-action button now matches the hero button style for visual consistency.

items.title: Redundant Desktop Sync card in How It Works
items.body: The dedicated Desktop Sync section already covers this capability in depth, so the duplicate card was removed to reduce repetition.

releases.headline: Ownership-check fixes on drop/finalize and sync/rename
releases.summary: Closes two ownership-bypass paths: drop/finalize now verifies the requesting user owns the bundle before accepting the finalize token, and sync/rename no longer treats anonymous bundles as unowned-and-therefore-writable.

items.title: `drop/finalize` enforces bundle ownership
items.body: Previously any API key with upload scope could finalize a bundle if it possessed the cryptographic finalize token. The route now requires the bundle's `ownerId` to match the authenticated user, providing defense-in-depth on top of token possession.

items.title: `sync/rename` null-ownerId bypass closed
items.body: Anonymous bundles (where `ownerId` is `null`) were skipping the ownership check because the guard used short-circuit evaluation (`ownerId && ...`). The condition is now `!ownerId || ...`, so the route rejects rename attempts against anonymous bundles regardless of which API key issues the request.

releases.headline: Deployment scripts and self-hosting platform submissions
releases.summary: Adds seven first-class deployment paths with ready-to-run scripts and submits HermitStash templates to six self-hosting marketplaces so operators can install from their native app stores.

items.title: Ubuntu/Debian native installer
items.body: One-liner `curl ... | sudo bash` installs Node.js 24, configures tmpfs, and registers a systemd unit for native (non-container) deployments.

items.title: Terraform module for DigitalOcean
items.body: Provisions a droplet, firewall rules, and optional DNS records for a turn-key cloud deployment.

items.title: Ansible playbook
items.body: Supports both Docker-based and native deployment modes against existing inventories.

items.title: Proxmox LXC template
items.body: Builds an unprivileged Debian 12 container with Docker installed, suitable for Proxmox homelab setups.

items.title: LXD / Incus deployment
items.body: Auto-detects whether LXD or Incus is present on the host and configures nested Docker accordingly.

items.title: Podman deployment
items.body: Supports rootless and rootful Podman, applies SELinux `:Z` volume relabeling, and generates a systemd unit for managed startup.

items.title: Hardened systemd unit
items.body: Ships a service file with `NoNewPrivileges`, `ProtectSystem=strict`, and related sandboxing directives for native deployments.

items.title: Self-hosting platform templates
items.body: HermitStash templates submitted to Umbrel, Cosmos Cloud, CapRover, Coolify, Portainer, and Unraid. A custom Runtipi app store is published at https://github.com/dotCooCoo/runtipi-appstore.

items.title: OCI `image.url` label points to https://hermitstash.com
items.body: Updates the container image metadata so registry UIs link to the project site rather than the previous placeholder URL.

items.title: README documents all deployment configurations
items.body: Each of the seven new deployment paths is documented with prerequisites, install commands, and post-install verification steps.

releases.headline: AGPL-3.0 relicense, Docker multi-arch images, settings schema, security fixes
releases.summary: Relicenses the project from MIT to AGPL-3.0-or-later, ships signed multi-architecture container images on GHCR with SBOM and provenance attestations, introduces a type-safe schema for all 74 admin settings, and closes an IDOR in the sync-rename path.

items.title: Multi-architecture container images on GHCR
items.body: `ghcr.io/dotcoocoo/hermitstash:1.6.0` ships for `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64`, with OCI labels, cosign signatures, SBOM and provenance attestations, Trivy scanning, and a SHA3-512 digest in the build summary. A post-publish smoke test exercises health check, non-root execution, version reporting, security headers, and graceful shutdown.

items.title: `/dev/shm` runtime usage monitor
items.body: Warns at 75% utilisation and errors at 90% so operators using tmpfs-backed scratch storage see exhaustion before uploads start failing.

items.title: `lib/settings-schema.js` — typed admin settings
items.body: Provides type-safe sanitisation and validation for all 74 admin settings, replacing ad-hoc per-route parsing.

items.title: `config.onReset()` registry
items.body: Cached clients (S3, mail, webhook signers, etc.) auto-invalidate when an operator updates settings, eliminating the class of stale-client bugs that surfaced as `SignatureDoesNotMatch` after S3 credential rotation.

items.title: `SecP384r1MLKEM1024` as top TLS group preference
items.body: Level 5 post-quantum hybrid group is now offered ahead of `X25519MLKEM768`. Preference order is centralised in `lib/constants.js` so future upgrades are a one-line change.

items.title: Pre-built image compose file and platform manifests
items.body: Adds `docker-compose.image.yml`, a full Kubernetes manifest (namespace, PVCs, deployment, service, ingress template), an Unraid Community Applications template, and a platform deployment guide in the README covering Coolify, Portainer, Synology, QNAP, and TrueNAS.

items.title: License relicensed to AGPL-3.0-or-later
items.body: All repositories in the project move from MIT to AGPL-3.0-or-later. Downstream redistributors and network-service operators must comply with the AGPL's source-availability requirements.

items.title: Config values trimmed on load and save
items.body: Whitespace around configuration values is stripped at both ingestion and persistence to prevent trailing-whitespace DNS and authentication failures that previously presented as opaque connection errors.

items.title: `/sync/rename` IDOR fixed
items.body: Any API key could previously rename files in any bundle regardless of ownership. The route now enforces bundle ownership before applying the rename.

items.title: HSTS preload directive added
items.body: The `Strict-Transport-Security` header now includes `preload`, allowing the deployment to be submitted to the browser preload list.

items.title: `ARGON2_FAST` startup warning
items.body: When the fast (test-only) Argon2id parameters are active in a production-shaped startup, a warning is emitted so the weakened cost is not silently in effect.

items.title: Per-chunk size limit on chunked uploads
items.body: Each individual upload chunk is now bounded, closing a memory-pressure vector where a single oversized chunk could be accepted within an otherwise compliant overall upload.
